title: [CI:DOCS] extend docs to include help for when pub/priv key is signing issue
body: extend docs to include help for when pub/priv key is signed with an unsupported algo

comments.body:
I ran into an issue on a fresh install of fedora 34. It wouldn't accept my rsa-sha signed key. I have no idea if this is ok how I've added the docs -- I am not a technical writer -- so I am totally open to feedback.

Also, not quite sure if this should be a seperate file that is linked to the original one? Is it too specific talking about Fedora?

/assign @rhatdan

Thinking about this some more -- I should probably add this to the troubleshooting doc but I do think it wouldn't hurt to have a link to that document in this one as well maybe under a Troubleshooting header.

You need to sign your commits, and to make sure the description is less then 80 Chars followed by a blank line.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *gigatexal*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@rhatdan how do I pass the "lgtm" label to the tide bot?

`tide Pending — Not mergeable. Needs lgtm label.`

We require two maintainer LGTMs to merge. After the first LGTM, another maintainer will review and, if they want to merge, command the bot proceed. For example:

/lgtm
